title: Labor Commons Specialist Record
description: Canonical schema for a labor-commons spec.yaml. Formalizes the structure already in use across the catalog's 651 existing records (schema_version, kind, freshness, metadata, purpose, scope, adjacent_specialties, knowledge_baseline) and additively defines two new blocks -- certification and seed_provenance -- introduced by the labor-commons-specialist-contract design. Existing records are valid without the new blocks; certification is populated over time by labor-commons-curator's backfill sweep, not required retroactively.

schema_version.description: Locked to '1.0' for every record currently in the catalog. A future breaking change to this schema bumps this value; consumers should treat an unrecognized version as unsupported rather than guessing.

kind.description: Fixed discriminator. Historical drift (agent_spec_pack, spec_pack) has reached main before -- any other value is invalid, not a variant to tolerate.

agent_id.description: Present in all 651 current records by convention but not enforced by the pre-existing validator. Kept optional here rather than silently required, to document reality rather than assert an unverified contract.

specialty_boundary.description: One-paragraph statement of what this specialist owns and rejects. 900-char floor matches the delivery contract in .github/copilot-instructions.md and infra/scripts/validate-spec-yaml.mjs (catalog median ~1,300).

status.description: draft/validated/published/flagged/stale/retired per the certification-gate design (labor-commons-curator/docs/data_model.md). Records predating that design use validated; flagged is set only by the backfill certification sweep on a failed retroactive check, never by hand.

adjacent_specialties.description: Slugs of real sibling specialists that exist elsewhere in the catalog. As of the labor-commons-specialist-contract design, this list is maintained on an ongoing basis by labor-commons-curator via LLM judgment over the catalog (not just set once at authoring time) -- see labor-commons-curator/docs for the adjacency-maintenance sweep. A slug that does not resolve to a real record is a defect, not a warning.

authority_sources.description: 8-source floor matches the delivery contract (catalog median 9).

location.description: The URL key is always `location`, never `url` -- a recurring drift the pre-existing validator flags because commons-board/commons-crew's consumption code only reads `location`.

certification.description: Additive, optional -- absent on records that predate the certification-gate design (labor-commons-curator/docs/data_model.md § CertificationResult) until labor-commons-curator's backfill sweep processes them. Never hand-authored: written only by labor-commons-curator's independent scenario-generation/grading pipeline, which must not share session/context with whatever authored this record's content (see data_model.md's independence requirement) -- generated_by and graded_by exist so that independence is auditable, not just asserted.

generated_by.description: Identifier for the scenario-generation pass, distinct from whatever authored this record.

graded_by.description: Identifier for the grading pass, distinct from both authoring and scenario-generation.

origin.description: pre_publish_gate: certified before this record was ever published. backfill_sweep: certified retroactively by labor-commons-curator's non-blocking sweep of records that predate the gate.

seed_provenance.description: Additive, optional -- present only on records from the one-time initial NAICS-based curation pass (see labor-commons-curator/docs/roadmap.md). Absent on every record created through ongoing growth (system-detected gap-filling or curated human-authored sourcing), which does not reference this structure.

automation_fit_rationale.description: Why this code was judged a good candidate at seed time, e.g. information/knowledge-heavy work or repetitive/process-driven work.
